app.phasesHint: Screens (boot → lobby → game → game over) arrive in phases.

start.description: A strategic card game about the real grind of software development. Bugs, surprise events, rivals' attacks — beat it all and release first.
start.lobbyNote: Лимит игроков и режимы партии настраиваются уже в лобби — пересоздавать ничего не нужно.

rulesContent.foot: Modes (hand limit, Fast Release, release condition, AI count) are chosen before the game — details will appear in the create-game dialog.

sections.title: Goal
body: Be the first to collect three different Release cards (Frontend, Backend, Database) in your release zone, fending off all opponents' attacks — or be the last player standing.

sections.title: Setup
body: Two decks by back color: green — main, purple — events.
Each player: 1 Debugger card + 4 random (5 in hand total).
AI and Error 503 cards are not allowed at the start — replace them in hand.
Shuffle and place both decks face down.

sections.title: Player turn
sections.note: action / draw / action / end of turn
body: Before and after the draw you may play any number of cards (including 0).
Draw — exactly one card from the top of the main deck (mandatory).
End of turn is announced with the word "PUSH"; play goes clockwise.

sections.title: Releasing cards
body: The release zone holds Release cards; one of each type.
Only one release per turn; placing one discards 1 card from hand.
A fresh release can be instantly answered by opponents with a lightning-icon attack (Bug, Out of Memory, Legacy Code, Security Bug).
Code Review is played together with a Release and makes it immune to those attacks (even with Sudo). It cannot be applied to an already-placed release.

sections.title: Attack and defense
body: Attack cards (lightning) are played instantly — on a release or against a hand.
Defense: Cancel (Hotfix, Rubber Ducky, PR Approved, Rollback) and Unicorn (Not a Bug, Works on my Machine).
Sudo boosts cards with a sudo effect; Cancel does not work against a boost, Unicorn does.
DDoS is the only attack against a protected release and Monitoring.

sections.title: Trigger cards
body: AI: on draw, reveal to everyone and play a random effect from the events deck.
Error 503: on draw, reveal to everyone and neutralize it (Debugger, Monitoring, or sacrifice a release) — otherwise you are out.

sections.title: End of game
body: The game ends when a player has three different Release cards in their release zone at once — or when only one player remains.

no.label: No AI
no.desc: Remove: all AI cards, 1 Error 503, 2 Debugger
